formulas.name: The Conflict Between Appearance and Reality
formulas.formula: Appearance can be deceiving, and reality may be different from what it seems.
formulas.variables: Appearance: what is seen or seems to be true; Reality: the actual situation or facts
formulas.notes: This theme is central to the play and highlights the contrast between the idealized image of Dr. Frayne and the harsh realities of his life.

formulas.name: The Significance of Time and Memory
formulas.formula: Time and memory can shape our perceptions and experiences, influencing how we see ourselves and others.
formulas.variables: Time: the passage of time; Memory: the ability to recall past events and experiences
formulas.notes: The play explores the themes of time and memory, highlighting how these factors can impact our understanding of ourselves and others.

formulas.name: The Power of Human Relationships
formulas.formula: Human relationships can have a profound impact on our lives, influencing our emotions, actions, and perceptions.
formulas.variables: Human relationships: interactions between people, including friendships, romantic relationships, and family bonds
formulas.notes: The play highlights the complex relationships between the characters, demonstrating how these relationships can shape our experiences and emotions.

formulas.name: The Impact of Class and Social Status
formulas.formula: Social class and status can influence our opportunities, experiences, and perceptions, shaping our lives in meaningful ways.
formulas.variables: Social class: the economic and cultural position of an individual or group; Social status: the level of respect or prestige accorded to an individual or group
formulas.notes: The play explores the themes of class and social status, highlighting how these factors can impact the characters' experiences and opportunities.

formulas.name: The Tension Between Tradition and Change
formulas.formula: Tradition and change can be in conflict, with tradition often representing stability and change representing uncertainty and upheaval.
formulas.variables: Tradition: established customs, practices, or values; Change: new ideas, actions, or circumstances
formulas.notes: The play explores the tension between tradition and change, highlighting how these opposing forces can impact the characters' lives and relationships.
